basics.name: Venkata Naresh Boddepalli
basics.label: Plant Breeder | Legume Specialist | Genomics, Phenomics & AI-Assisted Breeding
basics.summary: Results-driven plant breeder with ~12 years of experience in legume crop improvement, integrating classical breeding with GWAS, genomic prediction, and AI-assisted high-throughput phenotyping. Proven track record advancing breeding pipelines from crossing through variety release across South Asia and the USA, with hands-on expertise in mungbean, urdbean, vegetable soybean, and cowpea.

location.address: Department of Agronomy, Iowa State University

work.name: Iowa State University
work.position: Graduate Teaching Assistant – AGRON 5110 Crop Improvement
work.summary: Delivered guest lectures on GWAS, comparative genomics, and genomic prediction; graded assignments and facilitated student discussions.

work.name: Iowa State University – Dept. of Agronomy
work.summary: PhD research in legume genomics and AI-assisted phenomics under Dr. Arti Singh.
highlights: Introduced crossing protocols that improved hybridization success from 25% to 65%, shortening breeding timelines by 1–2 years.
Designed AI-enabled pod and seed imaging systems for automated phenotyping, reducing manual scoring time by 60%.
Conducted GWAS and genomic prediction on 372 mungbean genotypes (~30K SNPs).
Established mutation breeding platform using gamma rays and fast neutrons to generate novel genetic variation.
FAA Part 107 licensed drone pilot supporting field-scale imaging across multi-acre nurseries.
Standardized mungbean/urdbean BBCH growth stage charts for Midwest conditions, now used as a reference protocol by collaborators.

work.name: World Vegetable Center (AVRDC), ICRISAT Campus
work.position: Scientific Officer – Legume Breeding & Seed Production (South/Central Asia)
work.summary: Managed full breeding and seed production pipeline for 5 legume crops across South and Central Asia.
highlights: Managed full breeding pipeline for mungbean, urdbean, vegetable soybean, cowpea, and yard-long bean, delivering disease-resistant, higher-yielding lines to national partners.
Led and managed a team of 10 staff (4 on-campus, 6 field sites) across multiple concurrent breeding projects.
Trained 50+ researchers from 10 countries in mungbean breeding and KDDart database management; trained 100+ farmers on good agricultural practices.
Co-authored grant proposals securing ~USD 1 million (ACIAR, 2 phases) for mungbean improvement in South/Southeast Asia.
Contributed to RKVY-funded proposals securing ~USD 480K for legume productivity improvement in Odisha and Karnataka.
Introduced digital data capture workflows adopted by 10 partner institutions, improving data timeliness and cross-site comparability.
Served as regional gene bank coordinator managing seed increase, purity checks, and MTA/SMTA compliance.

work.name: Dr Rama Naidu Vignana Jyothi Institute for Rural Development
work.summary: Taught 7 credit hours covering breeding & seed production of horticultural crops, protected cultivation/forestry, and communication skills; mentored 12 diploma students through PAMP practicum.

work.name: Chandra Shekhar Azad University of Agriculture & Technology
work.summary: Conducted eggplant and garden pea crossing trials; screened brinjal hybrids for Cercospora leaf spot resistance and field peas for powdery mildew resistance.

volunteer.position: President & Co-chair, 13th Raymond F. Baker Plant Breeding Symposium
volunteer.summary: Led 7-person graduate student committee for symposium planning and execution.

volunteer.position: Agronomy Department Senator & Election Committee Member
volunteer.summary: Represented agronomy students and liaised with CALS Dean on student issues.

volunteer.position: Chair, Food & Venue Committee, 11th R.F. Baker Plant Breeding Symposium

Lead Trainer, KDDart Database Management – International Mungbean Improvement Network
volunteer.summary: Organized hands-on workshops in Myanmar and Bangladesh.
